title: Batch #2847 — Sterility Failure
description: Sterility test failure detected during final QC. Batch #2847 of Injectable Product X (50mL vials) failed USP <71> sterility testing conducted on 2024-11-15. Positive result for Gram-negative rod identified in 2 of 14 units tested. Organism tentatively identified as Burkholderia cepacia complex pending confirmation. Batch quarantined pending investigation. This is a critical GMP deviation with potential patient safety impact.
facility: Building A — Sterile Manufacturing, Grade A Fill Zone
regulatory_refs: 21 CFR 211.68 — Automatic, Mechanical, and Electronic Equipment
EU GMP Annex 1 — Manufacture of Sterile Medicinal Products
PDA TR13 — Fundamentals of an Environmental Monitoring Program
expected_root_causes: Filter integrity failure during sterile filtration
Environmental monitoring breach in Grade A zone
expected_capa_categories: Immediate containment — batch quarantine and customer notification assessment
Root cause investigation — filter integrity testing (bubble point, diffusion flow)
Root cause investigation — environmental monitoring data review (settle plates, air samples)
Root cause investigation — personnel gowning records and training logs
CAPA — enhanced environmental monitoring program with increased frequency
CAPA — revised filter integrity testing protocol with 100% pre-use and post-use testing
Training — sterile technique refresher for all Grade A zone operators

evidence_documents.description: Standard Operating Procedure for sterile filtration of injectable products
Complete batch manufacturing record for lot 2847
Environmental monitoring report for Q3 2024 covering Grade A/B zones
Prior CAPA for environmental monitoring deviation in Grade B zone (2023)
